tools.title: Create a bookable context
tools.description: Creates a bookable context — the managed bookable thing (an event, a venue_schedule like a tee sheet or restaurant service, or a resource like a simulator bay). Provide its kind, display name, and timezone (required); config defaults to empty and slug is optional. The owning organization is taken from your session — never send it in the body. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

kind.description: The kind of managed bookable thing. `event` is a projected spine event; `venue_schedule` is a recurring-capacity context (restaurant service, gym timetable, tee sheet — differing only in schedule-rule config); `resource` is a book-the-thing-itself context (simulator bay, private dining room). Closed enum — set at creation, immutable thereafter.

timezone.description: IANA timezone the context's schedule is computed in (required — slot times are meaningless without it).

config.description: Operator-provided config (slot duration, capacity, windows, pacing). Optional; defaults to {} server-side.

slug.description: Optional URL-friendly handle. Not unique — duplicates are accepted by the schema.

tools.title: Project a legacy event context
tools.description: Projects a real Sonik event into booking as bookable_context.kind=event using externalEventRef as a soft shadow key. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

name.description: Operator-facing display name copied from the legacy event.

timezone.description: IANA timezone the projected event context's schedule is computed in.

config.description: Optional event projection config envelope. Effectful booking behavior still belongs to schedules/resources/policies.

slug.description: Optional legacy event slug to mirror on the event context.

tools.title: Create a schedule rule
tools.description: Adds a schedule rule to a context. A rule is an open config envelope expressing an effective-date range, a priority, and pacing/window/turn-time settings (e.g. opens/closes, slotMinutes, capacity); higher-priority rules win where effective windows overlap. Rules are how availability is defined — availability itself is always computed from them, never stored. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

contextId.description: The bookable context's id. OpenAPI path parameter.

rule.description: The schedule-rule config envelope (required). See ScheduleRuleEnvelopeSchema for documented keys.

tools.title: Get computed availability for a context
tools.description: Returns the computed availability slots for a context over a date range (from/to). This is THE law of the booking framework: availability is computed from the context's schedule rules every time it is requested — it is never stored as slots. Each returned slot carries its start, end, and remaining capacity after existing bookings. Optional partySize/source/resource filters let the read hide slots that active booking-window or party policies would reject; the response remains a bare slot array without pricing or reason-code expansion. This read is NOT tracked as an attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

contextId.description: The bookable context's id. OpenAPI path parameter.

from.description: Start of the availability window (ISO 8601 date or datetime), inclusive. OpenAPI query parameter.

to.description: End of the availability window (ISO 8601 date or datetime), inclusive. OpenAPI query parameter.

partySize.description: Optional party size for policy-aware availability filtering. Defaults to 1 when omitted. OpenAPI query parameter.

source.description: Optional booking source for policy-aware availability filtering. Defaults to web when omitted. OpenAPI query parameter.

resourceTypeId.description: Optional resource type target for policy-aware availability filtering. When supplied with resourceUnitId, the unit must belong to this type. OpenAPI query parameter.

resourceUnitId.description: Optional resource unit target for policy-aware availability filtering. Implies the unit's resource type for policy matching. OpenAPI query parameter.

tools.title: Create a booking
tools.description: Creates a booking against a bookable context for a persistent user/guest over a time window. The same shape backs a tee time, a dinner reservation, or any other booking — the context decides what it means. Supply a clientRequestId to make the create idempotent: retrying with the same key returns the existing booking with 200 rather than creating a duplicate (the Worker-retry defense). The organization is taken from your session. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

contextId.description: The bookable_context to book against (FK → bookable_context.id).

userId.description: Persistent auth/user id for the person holding the booking. Use /api/v1/booking/guests to search or create this identity.

customerId.description: Optional/deprecated org-scoped customer-context id for imported CRM/marketing context. Never required for reservation creation.

anyOf.description: A booking-domain UUID (Postgres uuid primary/foreign key).

endsAt.description: Window end — must be strictly after startsAt (422 otherwise).

source.description: Channel the booking originated from (text + CHECK, schema.ts:122-125). Carried into the v4 attribution signal at capture time.

clientRequestId.description: Idempotency key. Retrying create with the same (org, clientRequestId) returns the EXISTING booking with 200, never a duplicate. Omit for server-originated rows. See BookingBookingsContractCompatibility.idempotency.

Provide a userId for a known guest, or omit it for a walk-in/guest — a guest without a user record is identified by displayName alone and is never written as CRM/customer profile data. tools.title: Post a charge, payment, or adjustment
tools.description: Operator op. Records one ledger entry on a member's tab: a charge (positive — they owe more, e.g. a tee time or dinner), a payment (negative — they paid the tab down), or an adjustment (either sign — a comp or correction). amount_cents must be nonzero. Optionally links the entry to the booking that produced it. Ledger-only: this records money, it does not capture or settle any payment (no payment rails are integrated). Intended as the future Layer-1 monetary-signal emission point (capture-only). Responds with the created entry. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

kind.description: Ledger entry kind (house_account_entry_kind_check): 'charge' (member owes more, positive), 'payment' (member paid down, negative), or 'adjustment' (comp/credit/correction, either sign).

amountCents.description: Signed entry amount in minor units; must be a nonzero integer. Charges positive, payments negative, adjustments either sign (see signConventions).

bookingId.description: Booking this entry is tied to (e.g. the tee time or dinner being charged), if any.

occurredAt.description: When the charge/payment occurred (ISO 8601); defaults to now() server-side (house_account_entry.occurred_at default).

accessibility.label: Post a charge, payment, or adjustment
accessibility.description: Operator op. tools.title: Read a member's statement / F&B-minimum progress
tools.description: Operator op. Returns a computed statement for a member's tab over an optional occurred-at period: the net balance, per-kind subtotals (charges, payments, adjustments), and an entry count. The charge subtotal over a billing period is the food-and-beverage-minimum progress number — how much the member has spent toward their minimum. Every figure is a read-time aggregate over the ledger; nothing here is stored. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

from.description: Inclusive period start (ISO 8601); omit for all-time. OpenAPI query parameter.

to.description: Exclusive period end (ISO 8601); omit for all-time. OpenAPI query parameter.

commands.title: Create a bookable context
commands.description: Creates a bookable context — the managed bookable thing (an event, a venue_schedule like a tee sheet or restaurant service, or a resource like a simulator bay). Provide its kind, display name, and timezone (required); config defaults to empty and slug is optional. The owning organization is taken from your session — never send it in the body. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

examples.title: Example input for Create a bookable context

kind.description: The kind of managed bookable thing. `event` is a projected spine event; `venue_schedule` is a recurring-capacity context (restaurant service, gym timetable, tee sheet — differing only in schedule-rule config); `resource` is a book-the-thing-itself context (simulator bay, private dining room). Closed enum — set at creation, immutable thereafter.

timezone.description: IANA timezone the context's schedule is computed in (required — slot times are meaningless without it).

config.description: Operator-provided config (slot duration, capacity, windows, pacing). Optional; defaults to {} server-side.

slug.description: Optional URL-friendly handle. Not unique — duplicates are accepted by the schema.

Closed enum — set at creation, immutable thereafter.

commands.title: Get computed availability for a context
commands.description: Returns the computed availability slots for a context over a date range (from/to). This is THE law of the booking framework: availability is computed from the context's schedule rules every time it is requested — it is never stored as slots. Each returned slot carries its start, end, and remaining capacity after existing bookings. Optional partySize/source/resource filters let the read hide slots that active booking-window or party policies would reject; the response remains a bare slot array without pricing or reason-code expansion. This read is NOT tracked as an attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Create a booking
commands.description: Creates a booking against a bookable context for a persistent user/guest over a time window. The same shape backs a tee time, a dinner reservation, or any other booking — the context decides what it means. Supply a clientRequestId to make the create idempotent: retrying with the same key returns the existing booking with 200 rather than creating a duplicate (the Worker-retry defense). The organization is taken from your session. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

userId.description: Persistent auth/user id for the person holding the booking. Use /api/v1/booking/guests to search or create this identity.

customerId.description: Optional/deprecated org-scoped customer-context id for imported CRM/marketing context. Never required for reservation creation.

anyOf.description: A booking-domain UUID (Postgres uuid primary/foreign key).

endsAt.description: Window end — must be strictly after startsAt (422 otherwise).

source.description: Channel the booking originated from (text + CHECK, schema.ts:122-125). Carried into the v4 attribution signal at capture time.

clientRequestId.description: Idempotency key. Retrying create with the same (org, clientRequestId) returns the EXISTING booking with 200, never a duplicate. Omit for server-originated rows. See BookingBookingsContractCompatibility.idempotency.

commands.title: Post a charge, payment, or adjustment
commands.description: Operator op. Records one ledger entry on a member's tab: a charge (positive — they owe more, e.g. a tee time or dinner), a payment (negative — they paid the tab down), or an adjustment (either sign — a comp or correction). amount_cents must be nonzero. Optionally links the entry to the booking that produced it. Ledger-only: this records money, it does not capture or settle any payment (no payment rails are integrated). Intended as the future Layer-1 monetary-signal emission point (capture-only). Responds with the created entry. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

examples.title: Example input for Post a charge, payment, or adjustment

kind.description: Ledger entry kind (house_account_entry_kind_check): 'charge' (member owes more, positive), 'payment' (member paid down, negative), or 'adjustment' (comp/credit/correction, either sign).

amountCents.description: Signed entry amount in minor units; must be a nonzero integer. Charges positive, payments negative, adjustments either sign (see signConventions).

bookingId.description: Booking this entry is tied to (e.g. the tee time or dinner being charged), if any.

occurredAt.description: When the charge/payment occurred (ISO 8601); defaults to now() server-side (house_account_entry.occurred_at default).

commands.title: Read a member's statement / F&B-minimum progress
commands.description: Operator op. Returns a computed statement for a member's tab over an optional occurred-at period: the net balance, per-kind subtotals (charges, payments, adjustments), and an entry count. The charge subtotal over a billing period is the food-and-beverage-minimum progress number — how much the member has spent toward their minimum. Every figure is a read-time aggregate over the ledger; nothing here is stored. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

commands.title: Create a bookable context
commands.description: Creates a bookable context — the managed bookable thing (an event, a venue_schedule like a tee sheet or restaurant service, or a resource like a simulator bay). Provide its kind, display name, and timezone (required); config defaults to empty and slug is optional. The owning organization is taken from your session — never send it in the body. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Update a bookable context
commands.description: Updates a context's mutable fields — name, config, and/or slug. The context's kind cannot be changed: a kind determines how its schedule rules are interpreted, so changing it would orphan the rule semantics. Send only the fields you want to change. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

accessible.label: Update a bookable context

commands.title: Create a schedule rule
commands.description: Adds a schedule rule to a context. A rule is an open config envelope expressing an effective-date range, a priority, and pacing/window/turn-time settings (e.g. opens/closes, slotMinutes, capacity); higher-priority rules win where effective windows overlap. Rules are how availability is defined — availability itself is always computed from them, never stored. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

accessible.label: Create a schedule rule
accessible.description: Adds a schedule rule to a context. A rule is an open config envelope expressing an effective-date range, a priority, and pacing/window/turn-time settings (e.g. opens/closes, slotMinutes, capacity); higher-priority rules win where effective windows overlap. Rules are how availability is defined — availability itself is always computed from them, never stored. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Get computed availability for a context
commands.description: Returns the computed availability slots for a context over a date range (from/to). This is THE law of the booking framework: availability is computed from the context's schedule rules every time it is requested — it is never stored as slots. Each returned slot carries its start, end, and remaining capacity after existing bookings. Optional partySize/source/resource filters let the read hide slots that active booking-window or party policies would reject; the response remains a bare slot array without pricing or reason-code expansion. This read is NOT tracked as an attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Create a booking

The same shape backs a tee time, a dinner reservation, or any other booking — the context decides what it means. Supply a clientRequestId to make the create idempotent: retrying with the same key returns the existing booking with 200 rather than creating a duplicate (the Worker-retry defense). The organization is taken from your session. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

commands.title: Post a charge, payment, or adjustment
commands.description: Operator op. Records one ledger entry on a member's tab: a charge (positive — they owe more, e.g. a tee time or dinner), a payment (negative — they paid the tab down), or an adjustment (either sign — a comp or correction). amount_cents must be nonzero. Optionally links the entry to the booking that produced it. Ledger-only: this records money, it does not capture or settle any payment (no payment rails are integrated). Intended as the future Layer-1 monetary-signal emission point (capture-only). Responds with the created entry. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

commands.title: Create a bookable context
commands.description: Creates a bookable context — the managed bookable thing (an event, a venue_schedule like a tee sheet or restaurant service, or a resource like a simulator bay). Provide its kind, display name, and timezone (required); config defaults to empty and slug is optional. The owning organization is taken from your session — never send it in the body. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Update a bookable context
commands.description: Updates a context's mutable fields — name, config, and/or slug. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Create a schedule rule
commands.description: Adds a schedule rule to a context. A rule is an open config envelope expressing an effective-date range, a priority, and pacing/window/turn-time settings (e.g. opens/closes, slotMinutes, capacity); higher-priority rules win where effective windows overlap. Rules are how availability is defined — availability itself is always computed from them, never stored. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

Rules are how availability is defined — availability itself is always computed from them, never stored. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Get computed availability for a context
commands.description: Returns the computed availability slots for a context over a date range (from/to). This is THE law of the booking framework: availability is computed from the context's schedule rules every time it is requested — it is never stored as slots. Each returned slot carries its start, end, and remaining capacity after existing bookings. Optional partySize/source/resource filters let the read hide slots that active booking-window or party policies would reject; the response remains a bare slot array without pricing or reason-code expansion. This read is NOT tracked as an attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

accessible.label: Get computed availability for a context
accessible.description: Returns the computed availability slots for a context over a date range (from/to). Each returned slot carries its start, end, and remaining capacity after existing bookings. Optional partySize/source/resource filters let the read hide slots that active booking-window or party policies would reject; the response remains a bare slot array without pricing or reason-code expansion. accessible.actionLabel: Get computed availability for a context

commands.title: Create a booking
commands.description: Creates a booking against a bookable context for a persistent user/guest over a time window. The same shape backs a tee time, a dinner reservation, or any other booking — the context decides what it means. Supply a clientRequestId to make the create idempotent: retrying with the same key returns the existing booking with 200 rather than creating a duplicate (the Worker-retry defense). The organization is taken from your session. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

commands.title: Confirm a booking

commands.title: Reschedule a booking

The new ends must be strictly after the new starts. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

Supplying clientRequestId makes retried adds return the existing party row instead of duplicating the roster. Returns the booking's current active party roster.

commands.title: Post a charge, payment, or adjustment
commands.description: Operator op. Records one ledger entry on a member's tab: a charge (positive — they owe more, e.g. a tee time or dinner), a payment (negative — they paid the tab down), or an adjustment (either sign — a comp or correction). amount_cents must be nonzero. Optionally links the entry to the booking that produced it. Ledger-only: this records money, it does not capture or settle any payment (no payment rails are integrated). Intended as the future Layer-1 monetary-signal emission point (capture-only). Responds with the created entry. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

Optionally links the entry to the booking that produced it. Ledger-only: this records money, it does not capture or settle any payment (no payment rails are integrated). Intended as the future Layer-1 monetary-signal emission point (capture-only). Responds with the created entry. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

commands.title: Read a member's statement / F&B-minimum progress
commands.description: Operator op. Returns a computed statement for a member's tab over an optional occurred-at period: the net balance, per-kind subtotals (charges, payments, adjustments), and an entry count. The charge subtotal over a billing period is the food-and-beverage-minimum progress number — how much the member has spent toward their minimum. Every figure is a read-time aggregate over the ledger; nothing here is stored. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

accessible.label: List organizer templates
accessible.description: Lists public capability-first organizer starter templates. Templates are bundle/config descriptors only; they do not create vertical runtime types and do not execute install mutations.

commands.title: Inspect an organizer template manifest
commands.description: Returns the full organizer template manifest for a public starter. The manifest describes capability bundles, bookable-context kinds, proof surfaces, and non-live unsupported seams without claiming production install readiness.

accessible.label: Inspect an organizer template manifest
accessible.description: Returns the full organizer template manifest for a public starter. The manifest describes capability bundles, bookable-context kinds, proof surfaces, and non-live unsupported seams without claiming production install readiness.

commands.title: Dry-run organizer template install
commands.description: Returns a deterministic install report for the template and organization slug. This endpoint is read-only: executedMutations is false, venueProductionReady is false, and install mutations remain guarded/deferred.

accessible.label: Dry-run organizer template install
accessible.description: Returns a deterministic install report for the template and organization slug. This endpoint is read-only: executedMutations is false, venueProductionReady is false, and install mutations remain guarded/deferred.

commands.title: Get organizer template browser handoff
commands.description: Returns the browser/manual-proof handoff generated from the same template dry-run report used by the CLI. Unsupported seams remain non-live and durable template-install tables remain deferred.

accessible.label: Get organizer template browser handoff
accessible.description: Returns the browser/manual-proof handoff generated from the same template dry-run report used by the CLI. Unsupported seams remain non-live and durable template-install tables remain deferred.

commands.title: List booking media assets
commands.description: Lists durable booking.media_asset catalog rows for the caller organization, optionally scoped to one bookable context. Assets remain served through authenticated Worker routes, not a public CDN.

accessible.label: List booking media assets
accessible.description: Lists durable booking.media_asset catalog rows for the caller organization, optionally scoped to one bookable context. Assets remain served through authenticated Worker routes, not a public CDN.

commands.title: Upload booking media asset
commands.description: Creates a durable booking.media_asset catalog row and uploads private context-scoped bytes into BOOKING_MEDIA_BUCKET. The mounted endpoint accepts multipart/form-data with contextId, role, and file; it also supports application/octet-stream with contextId/fileName/role query params for SDK and CLI proof.

accessible.label: Upload booking media asset
accessible.description: Creates a durable booking.media_asset catalog row and uploads private context-scoped bytes into BOOKING_MEDIA_BUCKET. The mounted endpoint accepts multipart/form-data with contextId, role, and file; it also supports application/octet-stream with contextId/fileName/role query params for SDK and CLI proof.

commands.title: Read booking media asset
commands.description: Returns durable JSON metadata for a private media asset after catalog org/context ownership checks. The mounted authenticated REST href can stream bytes when called directly without metadata=true; generated clients use this JSON-safe metadata operation.

accessible.label: Read booking media asset
accessible.description: Returns durable JSON metadata for a private media asset after catalog org/context ownership checks. The mounted authenticated REST href can stream bytes when called directly without metadata=true; generated clients use this JSON-safe metadata operation.

commands.title: Delete booking media asset
commands.description: Soft-deletes the durable booking.media_asset catalog row and deletes the private R2 object after verifying the asset belongs to the caller organization.

accessible.label: Delete booking media asset
accessible.description: Soft-deletes the durable booking.media_asset catalog row and deletes the private R2 object after verifying the asset belongs to the caller organization.

commands.title: Read booking media image variant
commands.description: Streams an authenticated, org-scoped Cloudflare Images variant for an image asset stored privately in BOOKING_MEDIA_BUCKET. The catalog row and session are checked before the Worker reads R2 bytes or returns a cached variant.

accessible.label: Read booking media image variant
accessible.description: Streams an authenticated, org-scoped Cloudflare Images variant for an image asset stored privately in BOOKING_MEDIA_BUCKET. The catalog row and session are checked before the Worker reads R2 bytes or returns a cached variant.
